back_translation: After two full years, Pharaoh was dreaming, and behold, he was standing by the Nile.
Behold, from the Nile came up seven cows, beautiful in appearance and fat of flesh, and they grazed in the reeds.
And behold, seven other cows came up after them from the Nile, ugly in appearance and thin of flesh, and they stood beside the cows on the bank of the Nile.
The cows ugly in appearance and thin of flesh ate the seven cows beautiful in appearance and fat. Then Pharaoh awoke.
He slept and dreamed a second time, and behold, seven heads of grain came up on one stalk, plump and good.
And behold, seven heads of grain, thin and scorched by the east wind, sprouted after them.
The thin heads swallowed up the seven plump and full heads. Then Pharaoh awoke, and behold, it was a dream.
In the morning, his spirit was troubled, and he sent and called all the magicians of Egypt and all its wise men. Pharaoh told them his dream, but no one could interpret it for him.
Then the chief of the cupbearers spoke to Pharaoh, saying, "I remember my offenses today.
Pharaoh was angry with his servants, and he put me in custody in the house of the captain of the guard, me and the chief of the bakers.
We dreamed a dream on one night, I and he; each man according to the interpretation of his dream we dreamed.
There with us was a young Hebrew, a slave of the captain of the guard, and we told him, and he interpreted our dreams for us, each man according to his dream he interpreted.
And it happened just as he interpreted to us — me he restored to my position, and him he hanged.
Pharaoh sent and called Joseph, and they hurried him from the dungeon. He shaved and changed his clothes and came in to Pharaoh.
Pharaoh said to Joseph, "I dreamed a dream, and there is no one to interpret it. But I have heard concerning you that you hear a dream and interpret it."
Joseph answered Pharaoh, saying, "It is not in me. God will give Pharaoh a peaceful answer."
Pharaoh said to Joseph, "In my dream, behold, I was standing on the bank of the Nile.
Behold, from the Nile came up seven cows, fat of flesh and beautiful in form, and they grazed in the reeds.
And behold, seven other cows came up after them, poor and very ugly in form and thin of flesh; I have not seen any like them in all the land of Egypt for ugliness.
The thin and ugly cows ate the first seven fat cows.
They went into their bellies, but it was not known that they had gone into their bellies; their appearance was as ugly as in the beginning. Then I awoke.
I saw in my dream, and behold, seven heads of grain came up on one stalk, full and good.
And behold, seven heads of grain, withered, thin, scorched by the east wind, sprouted after them.
The thin heads swallowed up the seven good heads. I told this to the magicians, but no one could declare it to me."
Joseph said to Pharaoh, "The dream of Pharaoh is one. What God is doing, He has declared to Pharaoh.
The seven good cows are seven years; the seven good heads of grain are seven years; the dream is one.
The seven thin and ugly cows that came up after them are seven years; the seven empty heads scorched by the east wind shall be seven years of famine.
This is the matter that I spoke to Pharaoh: what God is doing, He has shown Pharaoh.
Behold, seven years are coming of great abundance throughout the land of Egypt.
Seven years of famine shall arise after them, and all the abundance shall be forgotten in the land of Egypt, and the famine shall consume the land.
The abundance shall not be known in the land because of the famine that follows, for it will be very heavy.
The dream was repeated to Pharaoh twice because the matter is established by God, and God is hastening to do it.
Now therefore, let Pharaoh look out a man discerning and wise, and set him over the land of Egypt.
Let Pharaoh do this and appoint officers over the land, and take a fifth of the land of Egypt during the seven years of abundance.
Let them gather all the food of these good years that are coming, and let them store up grain under the hand of Pharaoh — food in the cities — and keep it.
The food shall be a reserve for the land for the seven years of famine that shall be in the land of Egypt, that the land may not be cut off in the famine."
The matter was good in the eyes of Pharaoh and in the eyes of all his servants.
Pharaoh said to his servants, "Can we find such a one as this, a man in whom the Spirit of God is?"
Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Since God has made known to you all this, there is none discerning and wise like you.
You shall be over my house, and according to your word all my people shall kiss; only the throne shall I be greater than you."
Pharaoh said to Joseph, "See, I have set you over all the land of Egypt."
Pharaoh removed his signet ring from his hand and put it on Joseph's hand, clothed him in fine linen garments, and put a gold chain around his neck.
He had him ride in the second chariot that he had, and they called before him, "Bow the knee!" And he set him over all the land of Egypt.
Pharaoh said to Joseph, "I am Pharaoh, but apart from you no man shall lift up his hand or his foot in all the land of Egypt."
Pharaoh called Joseph's name Zaphenath-paneah, and gave him Asenath daughter of Potiphera priest of On as wife. Joseph went out over the land of Egypt.
Joseph was thirty years old when he stood before Pharaoh king of Egypt, and Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh and passed through all the land of Egypt.
The land brought forth in the seven years of abundance by handfuls.
He gathered all the food of the seven years that were in the land of Egypt and put food in the cities — the food of the field of each city around it he put in its midst.
Joseph stored up grain like the sand of the sea, very much, until he stopped counting, for it was beyond number.
To Joseph were born two sons before the year of famine came, whom Asenath daughter of Potiphera priest of On bore to him.
Joseph called the name of the firstborn Manasseh, "For God has made me forget all my hardship and all my father's house."
And the name of the second he called Ephraim, "For God has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction."
The seven years of abundance that were in the land of Egypt came to an end.
The seven years of famine began to come, just as Joseph had said. There was famine in all the lands, but in all the land of Egypt there was bread.
All the land of Egypt was famished, and the people cried to Pharaoh for bread. Pharaoh said to all the Egyptians, "Go to Joseph; what he says to you, do."
The famine was over all the face of the land. Joseph opened all that was in them and sold to Egypt, and the famine was severe in the land of Egypt.
All the lands came to Egypt to buy from Joseph, because the famine was strong in all the lands.
